channel.title: AP News 全文 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: 德黑兰袭击巴林和科威特，美国再次空袭伊朗，进一步危及临时协议
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military launched a second round of airstrikes Saturday targeting Iran at PresidentDonald Trump’sdirection as Tehran hit both Bahrain and Kuwait, underscoring rising tensions that threatenthe interim deal between the two nationsto stop the war.
The attack on Kuwait early Sunday was the first since the two sides signed a deal that aimed to halt fighting, and came as a multinational maritime body overseen by the U.S. Navy said Saturday that it would expand a route near Oman in the strait to allow for both inbound and outbound traffic — likely setting up a new flashpoint with Tehran.
The U.S. military’s Central Command said it struck Iranian military “surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone storage facilities and minelayer capabilities” following an attack on a ship at sea early Saturday morning. That ship, the Panamanian-flagged tanker Kiku, carried crude oil for the state-run energy company of Qatar, a key negotiator between Iran and the U.S.
In a social media post, Trump said the U.S. had “struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations, and coastal radar sites, for violating the Cease Fire Agreement, AGAIN!” He warned of a point where the U.S. may no longer be able to be reasonable “and will be forced to militarily complete the job.”
“If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!” Trump wrote on Truth Social.
The incident follows a similar back and forth that occurred just days prior whenan Iranian drone struck a merchant vesseloff the coast of Oman on Thursday and the U.S. military retaliated with strikes.
According to ship tracking websites, the Kiku left a Qatari oil field in the middle of the Persian Gulf earlier in the week and was bound for a port in the United Arab Emirates that sits on the Gulf of Oman, just on the other side of the Strait of Hormuz.
It appeared to be attempting to use a route that was established near the coast of Oman that is serving as an alternative to the route sanctioned by Iran that runs through its own waters.
The U.S. military said that “Iran had a chance to honor the ceasefire agreement” but “elected not to” when its forces attacked the Kiku.
Iran state TV reported explosions in an area just north of the Strait of Hormuz.
After the U.S. strikes early Sunday, Kuwait’s military said air defenses intercepted incoming Iranian drones and missiles. It offered no immediate information on any damage. Kuwait is home to a major U.S. Army base.
Earlier on Saturday, a statement from Bahrain’s Foreign Ministry said a “number of Iranian drones” targeted the country. It called the attack “a flagrant threat to the security of citizens and residents.” There were no immediate reports of damage.
Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard earlier issued a statement carried by the state-run IRNA news agency saying it had targeted several locations “of the U.S. terrorist army in the region.” It did not name what areas were targeted.
Bahrain has been one of the strongest critics of Iran and is home to the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet. It just hosted U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio fora meeting of the Gulf Cooperation Council’s foreign ministers, which ended with a call for an end to Iran’s attacks and for the strait to be completely open.
Overnight into Saturday, the U.S. military’s Central Command said it had struck Iranian missile and drone locations and coastal radar sites.
U.S. Vice President JD Vance, who has led the negotiations with Iran, said on social media Friday night that Iran should “pick up the phone” if there are disagreements about the ceasefire agreement, “but violence will be met with violence.”
The U.S. and Iran are negotiating terms of the deal including issues such as getting ships through the strait that’s vital to global supplies of oil and natural gas and addressing the future ofIran’s nuclear program and stockpile of highly enriched uranium.
Under the interim deal, the two sides have 60 days to work out the details. Ending thefighting in Lebanonbetween Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group is a key part of the deal.
The British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said that a tanker was attacked Saturday in the strait, with the crew safe and no environmental damage reported. No one immediately claimed the strike, but suspicion fell on Iran.
Just after that report, the Joint Maritime Information Center, overseen by the U.S. Navy, said the route near Oman’s shore is expanding to allow for inbound and outbound traffic.
Iran has insisted that ships must obey its orders and warned it will start charging fees for transit through the strait. However, ships have been increasingly trying to leave the Gulf in recent days.
Ebrahim Azizi, who heads the Iranian parliament’s national security commission, wrote Friday that “the Strait of Hormuz is governed by Iran, so: Respect the rules.”
The U.S. and Gulf Arab states have rejected Iran’s demands. The strait is considered as an international waterway, despite being the territorial waters of Iran and Oman.
The Joint Maritime Information Center warned that the threat to ships was “substantial,” adding that “mariners are advised of the existence of mines and should expect a naval presence as clearance operations continue.”
The International Maritime Organization on Fridayhalted a new effort to evacuate shipssaid it won’t resume until there are guarantees that the other ships won’t be attacked. It said about 115 ships have been able to move out of the strait in recent days.
Toropin reported from Washington, Associated Press writer Ali Swenson in New York contributed to this report.

item.title: 伊朗在美国袭击后袭击巴林和科威特，并威胁停止谈判以结束战争
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran launched drone and missile attacks Sunday targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to U.S. airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic, and threatened a “complete halt” in negotiations to end the war if Washington continues its attacks.
Efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz without Iran’s direct oversight sparked the crossfire now gripping the region and have imperiled negotiations for a lasting ceasefire. A multinational maritime body overseen by the U.S. Navy said Saturday that it would expand a route near Oman for both inbound and outbound traffic, setting up a new flashpoint with Tehran.
The global community has long considered the strait an international passageway, despite its sitting in Iran and Oman’s territorial waters. In recent days, Iran has twice attacked vessels going through a route on the Omani side of the strait backed by a United Nations agency.
Iran insists that it alone must govern the strait, the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf that once carried a fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reiterated the claim during a state visit to Iraq on Sunday.
“Any interference in this matter, any attempt to establish new or separate arrangements from those currently being carried out by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to further complications, delay the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and increase the level of tension, just as over the past two nights we witnessed incidents in the Strait of Hormuz that led to an increase in tension and confrontation,” he said in Baghdad.
The United States and Iran are still debating the terms of an interim peace deal, including shipping arrangements through the strait, removing a U.S. blockade and sanctions and addressing the future ofIran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium. Under the memorandum of understandingsigned earlier this month, the U.S. and Iran have 60 days to iron out the details.
The strikes threaten to torpedo the deal before it can be finalized. Continued fighting in Lebanon, where an Israeli soldier was killed by Hezbollah fire early Sunday, has also threatened the agreement.
The Kuwaiti military said air defenses intercepted incoming Iranian drones and missiles Sunday morning, just after the U.S. strikes.
Kuwait, which hosts a major U.S. Army base, said it had detected and intercepted two ballistic missiles and there were no reports of injuries or damage.
Bahrain’s Interior Ministry said the Iranian strikes damaged a residential building near the international airport and no one was killed. The ministry released photos of an 8-story building, with the top floor destroyed, filled with rubble and its windows blown out.
Bahrain is home to the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet, whose base there came under repeated attack during the war. The damaged building on Sunday was not near the fleet’s headquarters, in downtown Manama.
Bahrain’s Foreign Ministry denounced what it called “a dangerous escalation that reveals that what Tehran is doing is not a passing act, nor an isolated incident, but rather a deliberate approach and a systematic pattern of repeated aggression.”
Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard claimed responsibility for both attacks.
The latest U.S. strikes came after the U.S. and Iran traded attacks earlier in the weekend.
The U.S. military’s Central Command said it struck Iranian military “surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone storage facilities and minelayer capabilities” on Sunday, following an attack on a ship at sea early Saturday morning. That ship, the Panamanian-flagged tanker Kiku, carried crude oil for the state-run energy company of Qatar, a key mediator between Iran and the U.S.
In a social media post, Trump said the U.S. had “struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations, and coastal radar sites, for violating the Cease Fire Agreement, AGAIN!” He warned of a point where the U.S. may no longer be able to be reasonable “and will be forced to militarily complete the job.”
“If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!” Trump wrote on Truth Social.
The incident follows a similar back-and-forth that occurred just days prior, whenan Iranian drone struck a merchant vesseloff the coast of Oman on Thursday, and the U.S. military retaliated with strikes.
Iran has consistently said the ceasefire must include a halt to fighting in Lebanon, where Israel has been battling the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group. Days after the U.S. and Israel launched the war against Iran in February, Hezbollah began firing at Israel in solidarity with its Iranian allies. Israel responded with an invasion that has occupied large swaths of southern Lebanon and displaced hundreds of thousands of people. Israel has said it will not withdraw its troops until Hezbollah is disarmed.
Last week, Israel and the Lebanese governmentsigned a framework agreementto end the conflict. But their deal did not include Hezbollah or Iran. Hezbollah hascriticized the dealand rejected calls to disarm.
On Sunday, Araghchi again said during a visit to Iraq that the U.S. must force Israel to halt attacks and withdraw. Israel occupies around 600 square kilometers (231 square miles) in southern Lebanon, which it says it needs as asecurity bufferto halt attacks from Hezbollah.
But sporadic clashes have continued between Israel and Hezbollah.
The leader of the Iran-backed group said Saturday that Hezbollah would continue fighting until Israel withdraws from Lebanon, adding that the group considers the Israeli-Lebanese agreement signed on Friday to be “nonexistent.”
The frequency of Israeli strikes in Lebanon has decreased significantly since the Iran-U.S. deal was signed in mid-June, but strikes have continued, killing at least one person Saturday, according to the Lebanese Health Ministry. Two separate Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon on Sunday morning — one in the town of Taybeh and another in the Nabatiyeh area, according to Lebanon’s National News Agency. There was no immediate word on casualties.
Overnight, Hezbollah militants killed an Israeli soldier in the village of Deir Siryan in southern Lebanon, according to the Israeli military. Hezbollah did not comment on the incident.
Lidman reported from Tel Aviv, Israel. Associated Press writer Sally Abou AlJoud in Beirut contributed to this report.

item.title: 瑞士、丹麦和捷克共和国打破高温记录，中欧热火朝天
item.description:
BERLIN (AP) — Temperatures soared to record highs from Switzerland to the Czech Republic and Denmark on Saturday, as aheat wave that baked western European countriesthis week moved to central and eastern parts of the continent.
Unusually high temperatures were recorded even in the Nordic countries not known for sweltering summers. Denmark’s Meteorological Institute reported a record 37 degrees Celsius (98.6 degrees Fahrenheit) in Ødum north of Aarhus — the warmest day since records there began in 1874.
In Switzerland, a record 38.8 C (101.8 F) was set in the city of Basel.
Germany’s famous Autobahn was overwhelmed, too, as temperatures were expected to hit 40 C (104 F). In two places outside Berlin, the concrete of the A2 burst due to the high temperatures and the highway had to be closed. Other highway damage was reported across the country, according to the German daily Bild.
Train operator Deutsche Bahn and other rail companies advised against all nonessential train travel this weekend.
“Germany’s transportation infrastructure is being severely affected by the record-breaking heat this weekend,” Deutsche Bahn said in a statement.
The Czech Republic also saw its hottest day on record, with 40.8 C (105.4 F) in the northern town of Doksany. Forecasters said it may still rise.
In the western German city of Dormagen, dozens of residents of a nursing home were evacuated for medical care due to dangerous heat conditions in the building.
The local fire department reported that temperatures inside the home had reached 35 C (95 F). Air conditioning is not widespread in Germany and many countries in Europe because the continent is largely unused to such oppressive heat.
A resident at the home died overnight, but it was not yet clear whether the heat was the cause, a city spokesperson told German news agency dpa.
In France, multiple towns in the east of the country saw their highest-ever temperatures Saturday, with some above 40 C (104 F) even though the worst of the heat wave was starting to pass in some regions.
Paris and 36 other regions, stretching from the center to the east and northeast, remained in the extreme-heat red zone on Saturday, down from a peak on Thursday of 72 regions that were under such warnings. The capital continued to see unrelenting pressure on its hospitals, with a second consecutive day of nearly 3,000 people seeking care in public hospital emergency rooms, about a third more than normal.
The Paris public hospital authority, AP-HP, said it activated its emergency response plan across all 38 hospitals to cope. Phone calls to its medical dispatch centers were up nearly 80% compared with the same period in 2025, it said.
Concerns that hospitals could be overwhelmed prompted the postponement of the Paris Pride march for LGBTQ+ rights on Saturday, and a three-day music festival was canceled.
The temperatures this week have been higher than those during a historic 2003 heat wave that was blamed for 15,000 heat-related deaths, many of them older people. The AP-HP’s director, Nicolas Revel, said he doesn’t expect as many deaths this time, at least in Paris hospitals, in part because treatment for overheating has since improved.
During another exceptionally hot summer last year, more than 5,700 deaths were attributed to heat, according to France’s public health authority.
“I think we’ll be situated, clearly, between 2025 and without necessarily reaching the catastrophic level of 2003. But we have to expect that there will still be many deaths,” he said.
In the U.K., sweltering conditions are expected to gradually ease this weekend though an amber warning — one step down from red — remained in place until Saturday night.
Britons struggled to cope this week as the record June temperature was smashed three days in a row. Friday was confirmed as the country’s hottest June day on record, with a provisional temperature of 37.3 C (99 F) recorded in eastern England.
It was more than 1 C hotter than the long-standing record for June heat in the U.K., set in the summer of 1976.
On Saturday, police said the bodies of a 22-year-old man and a 15-year-old boy were recovered from a lake and a river. The deaths bring the total number of U.K. heat-related fatalities this week to four.
Authorities in the U.K. have warned people to take extra care when swimming in unsupervised areas following the deaths of around 40 people in France over the past week.
In Italy’s capital, which remains under a red heat alert, tourists tried to cool off seeking shade near buildings and dunking their heads under public fountains. Street vendors were doing a brisk business selling bottled water, hats and sun umbrellas.
Some turned to Italian classics for relief.
“Gelato, pasta, because it’s tradition, but also fresh fruit, and ice cold drinks, that’s the best for this temperature,” said Isabella Dold, a tourist from Kempten, Germany.
On Saturday, Italy’s health ministry said 18 cities — including the most popular tourism hubs like Venice, Florence, Bologna and Milan — were on red alert due to danger posed by the high temperatures.
Anew studyfrom the World Weather Attribution, a Europe-based collaboration of scientists, reported Friday that the record-breaking heat and humidity in Europe this week would not have been possiblewithout climate change.
The rapid study found that the heat would have been virtually impossible just five decades ago, and is 200 times more likely today than it would have been 20 years ago.
André Corrêa do Lago, the president of the U.N. climate talks known as COP30, said the heat wave has “helped strengthen the perception of urgency of fighting climate change.”
“The fact that we are living with this amazing heat in London is a strong argument, we need to agree, that we have to take action as soon as possible,” do Lago told The Associated Press.
Hui reported from London and Leicester from Paris. Associated Press journalists Trisha Thomas in Rome, Suman Naishadham in Madrid and Karel Janicek in Prague contributed to this report.

item.title: 肯塔基州州长称暴雨引发洪水已造成4人死亡
item.description:
肯塔基州法兰克福（美联社）——肯塔基州州长安迪·贝希尔周六表示，肯塔基州雷暴引发的洪水已造成四人死亡，并宣布进入紧急状态，预计还会有额外降雨。
据国家气象局称，周六肯塔基州和印第安纳州部分地区因强降雨而发出山洪警报。
该机构周六下午晚些时候表示，印第安纳州西南部的部分地区已经下了 4 至 10 英寸的降雨，而且可能还会有更多降雨。贝希尔的办公室表示，预计到傍晚，该州部分地区的降雨量将达到 7 英寸。
他在社交媒体上表示，麦迪逊县有3人因洪水死亡，杰克逊县有1人死亡。
据麦迪逊县验尸官办公室称，肯塔基州里士满市的部分地区被洪水淹没，居民被困在家中，两名洪水受害者（一男一女）被发现死在自己的房子里。
验尸官办公室称，在列克星敦附近的泰茨溪路 (Tates Creek Road)，另一名受害者被从被洪水困住的车辆中救出。
麦迪逊县副验尸官卡洛斯·科伊尔表示，搜救队正在挨家挨户寻找受灾严重地区的遇难者。他说，有些地区仍然无法进入。
贝希尔在社交媒体上表示，麦迪逊县有“重要的道路被淹没在水下”。他还表示，至少 12 条国道因被洪水“无法使用”。
他在另一份声明中表示：“这是一次严重的洪水事件，救援队伍已经不得不从整个联邦的车辆和房屋中进行多次水上救援。”“随着更多的大雨持续到今晚晚些时候，我们需要人们保持警惕并避免开车，尤其是天黑后能见度有限的时候。”
在肯塔基州西北部，路易斯维尔郊外，布利特县应急管理官员要求乡村道路上的居民在水坝堤坝发生山体滑坡后撤离，以防万一。他们说，大坝正在坚守，没有迹象表明即将崩溃。
据国家气象局称，过去两天该地区降雨量约为 3 英寸。

item.title: 科罗拉多州和犹他州边境的山火扑灭时造成 3 名消防员死亡、2 人受伤
item.description:
犹他州比弗（美联社）——美国野地消防局周日报道称，在科罗拉多州和犹他州边境扑救火灾时，三名消防员死亡，两人受伤。
该机构于今年早些时候成立，旨在简化公共土地的灭火和减灾工作。该机构表示，消防员是周六对诺尔斯和戈尔火灾的跨机构响应的一部分。
“美国荒地消防局与美国农业部林业局团结一致，深感悲痛，坚定不移地支持那些留下的亲人。他们的勇敢、奉献和牺牲永远不会被忘记，”该公司在 Facebook 上发表的一份声明中表示。
美国西部地区的野火活动加剧，连续多日的炎热、干燥和大风天气导致犹他州、亚利桑那州和其他地区的火势加剧，该地区不断出现新的火灾。
最大的火灾是三叶杨大火，正在犹他州西南部崎岖的地形上燃烧。星期六，它穿过峡谷和山腰，摧毁了沿途的部分滑雪胜地和其他夏季小屋，面积扩大到超过144平方英里（373平方公里）。
比弗县当局于周六开始与消防队合作评估损失程度，但尚未立即做出估计。州长斯宾塞·考克斯在社交媒体上发帖称情况惨淡，但他感谢工作人员的“几次奇迹般的拦截和扑救”。
负责火灾的发言人艾莉莎·梅森 (Alyssa Mason) 表示，悬崖和陡坡让这项工作变得更加困难。
“很难将推土机和其他重型设备放入其中。也很难将发动机放入其中，”她说。“这并不会让交火变得不可能，但它确实会减慢速度。”
数百名消防员已抵达这个干旱的州，应对新的火灾以及由于预报员所说的关键火灾天气（危险的低湿度、温暖的气温和阵风）而不断增加的火灾。
由于犹他州的积雪量创历史新低，并且冬季是有记录以来最温暖的，因此今年的危险更加严重。据国家跨部门消防中心称，西方大部分地区正在努力应对类似的情况。
从阿拉斯加到佛罗里达，工作人员周六努力扑灭数十起火灾，其中三打被列为大型且未得到控制的火灾。
自今年年初以来，全国范围内已有近 300 万英亩（120 万公顷）土地被烧毁。这高于 10 年平均水平。
犹他州的情况非常严峻，以至于州长斯宾塞·考克斯在本周早些时候宣布进入紧急状态，并为该州在七月四日假期之前禁止燃放烟花扫清了道路。该命令发布之际，犹他州正在经历近代历史上最严重的野火季节之一，而历史性的干旱状况更是加剧了这种情况。
州官员表示，过去一周，犹他州野火发生次数有所增加，每场火灾都表现出前所未有的行为。州林务员杰米·巴恩斯 (Jamie Barnes) 表示，这些行动的启动增强了该州的荒地消防能力。
科罗拉多州州长贾里德·波利斯也于周六宣布进入紧急状态，并授权动用国民警卫队来扑灭火灾。
最近几天，国家气象局的预报员一直在向从加利福尼亚州到亚利桑那州和新墨西哥州的西部大片地区发出危险信号警告。
在大峡谷国家公园以南，当局表示，周六，一场新的野火的火焰正从大峡谷村和附近的图萨扬社区移开。但在约 50 英里（80 公里）外，另一场火灾促使科科尼诺县官员向肯德里克山附近的人们发出疏散命令。
周六，亚利桑那州北部部分地区停电，为该地区提供服务的公用事业公司启动了安全关闭，希望减少野火风险。
随着野火风险的扩大，断电在西方变得更加普遍。这通常是公用事业预报员权衡持续风速和阵风速度、可用燃料和地形等因素后的最后手段。
由于犹他州持续存在极端火灾情况，落基山电力公司还关闭了为比弗县和其他地区提供服务的电源线。
蒙托亚·布莱恩从新墨西哥州阿尔伯克基报道。美联社达拉斯撰稿人杰米·斯坦格尔；朱莉·卡尔·史密斯 (Julie Carr Smyth)，俄亥俄州哥伦布市；底特律的埃德·怀特 (Ed White) 也做出了贡献。

item.title: 一项新法律可能会列出非法居住在密西西比州的移民名单。倡导者感到震惊
item.description:
A new Mississippi law will authorize the state’s top law enforcement agency to compile a list of all immigrants illegally living in the state.
What’s to be done with that information is a bit open-ended. But the law set to take effect Wednesday is sparking alarm among immigrant advocates, who fear it could become a new tactic to target immigrants in conjunction with President Donald Trump’splan to deportmillions of people lacking legal approval to live in the U.S.
The lawsays the state Department of Public Safety “may use all reasonable lawful investigative means available” to determine the number and identities of all “illegal aliens” in Mississippi. That includes collecting their names, addresses, country of origin and whether they are an adult or minor. It also includes noting any criminal history and the date, location and status of deportation proceedings.
The department is directed to share information on those suspected of violating laws with state and local authorities. The measure neither requires nor prohibits the database from being shared with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
Republican state Sen. Angela Hill, who sponsored the measure, said states have a right and obligation to assist the U.S. government in discouragingillegal immigration, which she said facilitates crimes such as human and drug trafficking.
The new law “seems like commonsense to me,” Hill said. “In order to address the problems caused by illegal immigration, we need to understand the magnitude of the problem. Identifying the number and identity of illegal aliens in Mississippi is a concrete way to better understand the problem.”
Nationwide, states already have enacted more than 100 immigration-related laws this year, according to an Associated Press tally.
In Republican-led states, those measures generally have aligned withTrump’s agendaby requiring local sheriffs to sign cooperative agreements with ICE, reinforcing eligibility restrictions for public benefits and directing election clerks to check voter rolls against the federalSystematic Alien Verification for Entitlementssystem in an attempt to flag noncitizens.
Democratic-led states generally have pushed back against Trump with new lawsbanning cooperative pactswith ICE, forbidding ICE tactics likewearing masksand restricting immigration enforcement actions in schools, hospitals and other sensitive locations without judicial warrants.
The closest thing to Mississippi’s new law appears to bea 2021 executive orderby Republican Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis. That measure directed the Florida Department of Law Enforcement to “use all lawful investigative means available” to determine the number and identities of all “illegal aliens” who had been transported from the nation’ssouthwest border to Florida.
The Florida agency did not respond to an AP request for information about the results of the executive order.
Trump’s administration, meanwhile, has stepped up enforcement of a decades-oldfederal lawthat requires noncitizens toregister with the U.S. government.
The Mississippi law envisions more than a one-time count. It prescribes an ongoing effort to keep track of immigrants illegally in the state for the next two years. That could get complicated as people overstay visas, apply for new forms of legal status and move into and out the state.
“You can be undocumented today, and then have status tomorrow, and then lose it again next month, and then regain it three months from now,” said Efrén Olivares, vice president of litigation and legal strategy at the National Immigration Law Center, a nonprofit that advocates for low-income immigrants.
“It’s practically unworkable, but it’s also very worrisome, because it’s eerily reminiscent of other countries that have created lists of certain groups of people,” Olivares said.
State officials will need to come up with “a credible and fairly foolproof way of correctly determining someone’s immigration status,” said Jessica Vaughan, director of policy studies at the Center for Immigration Studies, a nonprofit think tank that supports restrictions on immigration.
But Vaughan said the law “makes a lot of sense,” adding that it “raises the likelihood that someone’s illegal presence is going to come to the attention of federal authorities.”
Mississippi has one of the country’s smallest percentages of immigrants illegally residing in the state — fewer than 28,000 people, amounting to less than 1% of its population — according to a report by the American Immigration Council, which used 2023 Census Bureau data.
The new law “is very concerning for a bunch of different reasons,” including the potential to redirect law enforcement resources away from protecting the public to investigating people from foreign countries who may be contributing to the economy, said Victoria Francis, deputy director of state and local initiatives for the American Immigration Council, a nonprofit that advocates on behalf of immigrants.
“A mandate like this invites profiling and turning entire communities into targets,” Francis said.
The law could undermine trust between police and residents, said Lydia Grizzell, policy and advocacy manager for the American Civil Liberties Union of Mississippi.
“That increases the likelihood of individuals not reaching out to law enforcement when it’s needed – and that is opposite of the mission,” she said.

item.title: 在伟大的美国州博览会上，您可以找到恐龙的肋骨。团结是另一回事
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——恐龙的肋骨。一座重 7,000 磅的沙堡，由泽西海岸的沙子制成。一个无人值守的摊位上有两把摇椅，还有一些空位。
该博览会于本周开幕，由 Freedom 250 主办，该组织是唐纳德·特朗普总统创建的，旨在举办其政府的官方活动。它的创建引起了与 America250 的紧张关系，America250 是一个国会创建的组织，旨在策划美国建国 250 周年纪念活动。国家广场上的活动将持续到七月。
周五早些时候，人群数量很少，在安检上花费的时间很少。入口通道直通 110 英尺高的摩天轮（博览会上唯一的游乐设施）和一个较小的拱门，类似于共和党总统用来装饰其他活动的拱门。
接受采访的人一致认为，这次展会是个好主意，但也许是错失了一个机会。
参加过集市的人都熟悉山羊、绵羊和小牛，它们的哞哞声在整个购物中心都能听到，这一切都是由来自西弗吉尼亚州杰斐逊县的美国华盛顿高中未来农民组织提供的。小鸡们的毛茸茸的柔软性闪闪发光，非常吸引人，并且有望与狂野的野马一起进行牛仔表演，以突显美国牛仔的崛起。
参观者们对各国聚集在一起并展示其独特的人物、著名的土著、工业、历史和地理多样性的热情团结在一起。他们还被对无党派庆祝美国团结的渴望所吸引。
“这里有友善的人、美好的活动、美好的家庭活动，”莎琳·博瓦特 (Sharyn Bovat) 说，她说她住在佛罗里达州，但自从今年心脏病发作后一直留在华盛顿地区。“这个国家的一半与另一半是分裂的。我希望他们能为所有人创造一个美国 250。我厌倦了政治。”
她说拱门是给这次活动增添政治色彩的主要因素。“这让我想起了德国，”她说。
展会展位的内部结构都类似于附近国家美术馆的新古典主义建筑，为参观者提供了美国风情的片段。
代表蒙大拿州的物品中有一个巨大的胸腔，是迷惑龙的放大版，是更知名的雷龙的近亲。
弗吉尼亚州的展位展示了该州在美国历史上的角色。哥伦比亚特区有一面巨大的横幅供游客签名，旁边还有一棵开满樱花的树。南卡罗来纳州邀请人们去享受果岭。
德克萨斯州包括一个太空舱、阿拉莫的正面和一座雕像——大德克萨斯州。外面排队的地方是亚利桑那州的展览，那里有一个互动装置，让游客可以将自己置身于该州的地形中。
参观新泽西州展位的参观者看到了一座华丽的沙堡，由一位艺术家用 7,000 磅从泽西海岸带来的沙子在四天内建造而成。
安迪·沃尔特斯 (Andy Walters) 和他的妻子克尔斯滕 (Kirsten) 带着来自俄亥俄州沃帕科内塔 (Wapakoneta) 的三个孩子来到这里，那里是阿波罗 11 号宇航员尼尔·阿姆斯特朗 (Neil Armstrong) 童年的家乡。“我认为这很好。我希望所有州都参与其中，”安迪·沃尔特斯说。“这有点令人沮丧，但这是一个好主意。”
克尔斯滕·沃尔特斯 (Kirsten Walters) 表示，她希望类似的活动能更频繁地举行，但不要有任何暗示。“我们很高兴来到这里并参与其中，”她说。
大多数州都参加了，将博览会几乎视为旅游机会。但没有充分的代表性。
缅因州、马萨诸塞州和宾夕法尼亚州以成本、日程安排或政治化问题为由，选择不派遣代表团。宾夕法尼亚州参议员约翰·费特曼（民主党人）和戴夫·麦考密克（共和党人）周六表示，他们已与几个商业贸易组织会面，“确保基斯通州将有一个值得展示其在我们国家历史上独特地位的展示。”
一些展位，包括夏威夷和阿拉斯加的展位，无人值守。夏威夷确实有几把摇椅，疲惫的游客可以充分利用它们。
来自纽约州罗马的小阿隆佐·刘易斯 (Alonzo Lewis Jr.) 和凯利·多米齐奥 (Kelly Domizio) 趁机减轻了负担。
“这真的很酷，”她说。“去过所有不同的州。”
“有必要吗，我不这么认为，”刘易斯说。“感觉很被迫。有太多的分离。”
多米齐奥说她还记得 1976 年的二百周年纪念。
她说，“有一种自豪感”和团结感。“我们很享受这一天，但感觉很被迫。”

item.title: 长期以来吸引游客的倒影池现在在特朗普的领导下受到了警方的审查
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——林肯纪念堂倒影池有很多意义。
浑浊。和平。臭。抗议、守夜、庆祝活动和令人惊叹的日出照片的背景。受到蚊虫和藻类的困扰。适合跑步的风景。首都精心设计的纪念性核心的关键部分。《阿甘正传》中标志性场景的拍摄地。
但有一件事通常不是：严格执行的警察区域。
进入泳池一直是非法的，但一般来说，涉水者最可能期望的是警察指示他离开。
但自从唐纳德·特朗普总统上周末在没有提供证据的情况下坚称破坏者对泳池内衬的损坏负有责任以来，这种情况发生了变化，在他指责前任总统忽视恶化后，破坏了他的翻修工作。本周提交的法庭文件显示，国家公园管理局向美国公园警察报告了 6 月 9 日发生的一起事件，其中一把尖刀或剃刀割破了泳池的新衬垫。
国民警卫队成员和公园警察在泳池周围的甲板上巡逻。美联社证实，一名男子在触摸已经剥落的油漆后被捕。他说他想检查一下新涂层，短暂地触摸了仍然附着的一块，然后在公园工作人员告诉他后不久就放开了。本周的某个时候，人们看到工作人员在该地区附近添加了围栏，政府将此归因于为七月四日庆祝活动做准备。
以下是多年来人们如何与倒影池互动的照片。
2016 年 5 月 31 日，捷克共和国布拉格的马丁·齐克 (Martin Zich) 在华盛顿国家广场的倒影池拍照后从水中涉水而出。（美联社照片/Andrew Harnik，档案）
仔细检查掩盖了这样一个事实：倒影池一直对游客具有吸引力。
1968 年 6 月 19 日，在华盛顿林肯纪念堂举行的团结日活动期间，许多穷人运动的支持者涌入倒影池。（美联社照片）
1968年的穷人运动期间，游泳池缓解了夏季的炎热。
在严寒的冬天，它变成了一个不太可能的城市溜冰场。
现在，这里是移动监视塔的所在地，执法人员徒步巡逻也有所增加，而纳米气泡的嗡嗡声则点缀着六月的空气。

item.title: 特朗普表示，他将提名前俄克拉荷马州警官兰斯·施罗耶担任 ICE 局长
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——唐纳德·特朗普总统周六表示，他将提名前俄克拉荷马州警官兰斯·施罗耶担任下一任移民和海关执法局局长。
特朗普在他的“真相社交”平台上表示，他任命的移民执法机构新人选是一名前美国海军陆战队员，也是一位“具有实际操作经验的爱国者”。他称施罗耶是一位“久经考验的领导者，拥有数十年锁定最糟糕情况的经验”。
施罗耶与新任国土安全部部长、前国会议员马克韦恩·穆林来自同一个州。本月早些时候，穆林在国家警长协会的一次活动中将施罗耶带到了舞台上，称他为“我的好朋友”，并指出国土安全部最近雇用了他。
周六，穆林在一份声明中迅速赞扬了施罗耶，强调了这位前士兵 29 年的职业生涯以及他与联邦和州合作伙伴在美国移民执法计划上的合作。
“特朗普总统做出了一个很好的选择，我相信兰斯的强大领导力和第一手经验将使 ICE 的男女人员能够驱逐非法外国人、保卫祖国并保护美国人民，”穆林说。
如果得到确认，施罗耶将领导 ICE，此时公众情绪因特朗普的移民镇压而恶化，特朗普的移民镇压导致大量联邦移民官员进入美国城市围捕移民。这些突袭行动导致紧张局势加剧，并引发抗议者与执法部门之间的冲突，导致今年早些时候明尼阿波利斯两名美国公民被枪杀。
2026 年 5 月 30 日星期六，新泽西州纽瓦克，一名支持移民和海关执法人员的抗议者走在德莱尼霍尔拘留中心外的路上（美联社照片/Angelina Katsanis）
特朗普带着大规模驱逐出境的承诺重返白宫，而 ICE 一直是这一愿景的核心执行者。该机构去年一次性注资 750 亿美元，目前正在经历大规模增长，这使得该机构能够雇用 12,000 名警官并增加拘留能力。
穆林于三月份上任，他承诺不会让他的部门成为头条新闻，并在移民问题上表现出温和的态度，尽管预计他会与总统关于大规模驱逐出境的优先事项保持一致。
ICE 前高级官员克莱尔·特里克勒-麦克纳尔蒂 (Claire Trickler-McNulty) 表示，之前确认的 ICE 主管通常都是律师，不过一些州和地方执法官员也被提名。她说他在俄克拉荷马州的背景表明穆林可能对选秀产生了影响。
国土安全部部长马克韦恩·穆林 (Markwayne Mullin) 于 2026 年 6 月 25 日星期四在华盛顿国会山众议院拨款委员会国土安全小组委员会作证。（美联社照片/何塞·路易斯·马加纳）
“我认为，考虑到 ICE 的关注，他可能希望感觉自己有一个可以信任的人，”她在接受采访时说。
ICE 的另一位高级官员约翰·托雷斯 (John Torres) 表示，施罗耶在获得参议院确认方面面临着艰难的过程，但他在州和地方层面而不是联邦层面的经验可能会有所帮助。
托雷斯说：“他不会有任何包袱，他们会转身说，哦，好吧，他为这届政府或那届政府工作过。”
施罗耶的提名是在前 ICE 主管托德·莱昂斯 (Todd Lyons) 于 5 月底辞职后获得的。大卫·文图拉 (David Venturella) 是一家私人监狱运营商的前高管，目前一直担任该机构的代理负责人。一位不愿透露姓名的国土安全部官员表示，在施罗耶获得参议院批准之前，文图拉预计将继续担任代理主任。
自奥巴马政府以来，ICE 就没有过参议院确认的主任，这是围绕该机构和移民政策的政治两极分化的结果。
美联社撰稿人埃利奥特·斯帕加特和丽贝卡·桑塔纳为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 比尔拒绝向 O.J. 致敬辛普森搬迁到新体育场
item.description:
纽约州果园公园（美联社）——布法罗比尔队即将离开 O.J.首席运营官皮特·古利（Pete Guelli）周六表示，辛普森及其玷污的遗产在本赛季搬迁到了新体育场。
在这位已故名人堂跑卫的名字仍保留在他们现在的故居内的名人墙上后，古利表示，在街对面的新体育场，不会再提及辛普森。
圭利在球队发布的声明中表示：“我们做出了组织决定，他不适合在我们的新体育场和家庭圈内展示。”
比尔队并没有复制他们的名人墙，而是计划通过在新体育场前一个名为“家庭圈”的大型聚集区展示他们的伟人来纪念他们的过去。
尽管辛普森在 20 世纪 70 年代在布法罗的九年职业生涯中创造了多项球队冲球记录，并成为 NFL 第一位单赛季冲球码数突破 2,000 码的球员，但辛普森的声誉因他在 1994 年杀害前妻及其男性朋友的指控而永远受到玷污。
尽管辛普森被判无罪释放，但后来在另一起民事案件中被判对死亡负责。后来，辛普森因不相关的指控被定罪，入狱九年。
2024 年 4 月，辛普森因前列腺癌去世，享年 76 岁。
比尔队在已故老板拉尔夫·威尔逊的领导下，很早就因谋杀指控而与辛普森保持距离。2014 年 Terry 和 Kim Pegula 购买了该特许经营权后，情况也是如此。
虽然辛普森仍然偶尔会通过个人关系参加布法罗主场比赛，但球队不会承认他的存在。
比尔队的前体育场，称为海马克体育场，正在拆除中。上周，该球队举行了剪彩仪式，为其耗资 21 亿美元的新设施（也称为海马克体育场）启用举行了剪彩仪式。

item.title: 凭借奥地利最后一秒的进球，伊朗队以微弱优势淘汰了动荡的世界杯
item.description:
伊朗队在世界杯上被淘汰，在一场充满政治色彩的世界杯小组赛中，伊朗队在美国施加的严格限制下进行了比赛。
伊朗队以一分之差无缘32强，令人心碎。
与比利时、新西兰和埃及战平，积三分，获得 G 组第三名。周六晚上，阿尔及利亚在伤停补时阶段打进一粒令人惊叹的进球，以 3-2 领先奥地利，伊朗似乎通过决胜局晋级，但几秒钟后奥地利在比赛的最后一场比赛中将比分追平。他们的平局确保了伊朗队被淘汰。
这是伊朗队在世界杯上最后一个痛苦的时刻，无论是场内还是场外都充满了混乱。
当德黑兰与华盛顿就永久结束今年早些时候开始的战争的协议进行谈判时，伊朗人一直在玩。周六，紧张局势仍在继续，伊朗对美国海军第五舰队所在地巴林发动无人机袭击，这可能是对美国夜间空袭的回应。几小时后，美国称伊朗袭击了霍尔木兹海峡附近的一艘船只，随后袭击了多个伊朗军事目标。
世界杯期间，伊朗教练阿米尔·加勒诺伊和球员抱怨了许多复杂情况，包括旅行限制、支持人员签证被拒以及赛后迅速离开美国。
美国官员表示，所有限制措施在比赛之前就已知晓。
美国和以色列于 2 月 28 日向伊朗发动战争，伊朗则在该地区发动袭击并控制霍尔木兹海峡进行报复。
今年三月，伊朗寻求将小组赛移至与其有外交关系的墨西哥举行。其将大本营从亚利桑那州图森迁至蒂华纳的请求在球队抵达前两周获得批准。
伊朗队周六晚上被淘汰后，球队发表了一份声明，表达了“对优秀的墨西哥人民，特别是美丽的蒂华纳市的衷心感谢”。
声明称：“离开蒂华纳对我们所有人来说确实很困难。”
在第一场比赛中，数百名伊朗裔美国人在体育场外抗议，呼吁德黑兰进行变革，并挥舞着革命前的狮子和太阳旗。数千人涌入体育场观看他们的比赛，赛前国歌响起，欢呼声和嘘声交织在一起。
在洛杉矶附近的前两场比赛中，球队直到前一天才被允许出行，并且必须在每场比赛结束后立即返回墨西哥。美国然后放松了限制，允许球队在周五对阵埃及的比赛前两天前往西雅图。如果伊朗晋级，下一场比赛将在不列颠哥伦比亚省温哥华进行。
“我们受到了非常非常糟糕的对待，”加哈诺伊在周五与埃及战平后表示，伊朗人仍抱有进入下一轮的希望。“我希望世界能够意识到这些问题。”
“这些年轻的伊朗国家队球员所做的事情应该被载入史册，”加勒诺伊说。“为什么？因为主人用最糟糕的方式对待我们。”

item.title: 梅西连续七场世界杯进球，刷新进球纪录
item.description:
德克萨斯州阿灵顿（美联社）——莱昂内尔·梅西没有太多时间尝试成为连续七场世界杯比赛中第一个进球的人。
无论如何，这位阿根廷巨星在下半场替补出场时做到了这一点。
周六晚上，阿根廷队在小组赛决赛中以 3-1 战胜约旦队，梅西打入第 19 球，连续七场进球，同时延续了男子世界杯进球纪录。
三天前梅西39岁生日后的第一场比赛中，梅西在第80分钟在禁区外被放倒后踢出任意球。几乎高于草地表面的低踢将两名乔丹防守球员劈入球网左角。
这是他职业生涯第 72 个任意球进球，其中包括他为阿根廷队攻入的第 12 个任意球进球。梅西目前在 202 场比赛中打进了 123 个国家队进球，位列历史第二，仅次于克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多 (Cristiano Ronaldo) 的 145 个进球。
梅西是仅有的三名连续六场世界杯进球的球员之一，另外两位是法国前锋贾斯特·方丹和巴西巨星雅伊尔津霍。
“你所看到的，我也看到了同样的事情，”教练莱昂内尔·斯卡洛尼通过翻译说道。“每次有人问起问题时，我都会感到有点不舒服，因为我不知道该说什么。”
梅西还在2014年世界杯对阵尼日利亚的比赛中打入任意球，他是自1966年以来在世界杯上打进两粒任意球的六名球员之一。他加入了贝利、里维利诺、特奥菲洛·库比利亚斯、伯纳德·根吉尼和大卫·贝克汉姆的行列。
“我为他感到非常高兴，就目前而言，”乔瓦尼·洛塞尔索在翻译中说道，他成为除梅西之外第一个在本届世界杯上进球的阿根廷人，也是在第19分钟的任意球。“事实是，每天看到他都会让人非常兴奋、兴奋和感染。所以显然这样看到他对我们来说非常重要。”
梅西没有首发是因为阿根廷已经锁定了J组第一名，而乔丹已经被淘汰出局，这场比赛就更没有意义了。
尽管如此，在 NFL 达拉斯牛仔队主场的 70,649 名绝对支持阿根廷的观众却渴望见到他。
下半场一开始，球迷们就开始高呼梅西的名字，当他替补出场热身时，球迷们大声欢呼。
2026 年 6 月 27 日星期六，德克萨斯州阿灵顿，达拉斯附近，阿根廷队的莱昂内尔·梅西 (Lionel Messi)（10 岁）在约旦队与阿根廷队之间的世界杯 J 组足球比赛中庆祝球队的第三个进球。（美联社照片/托尼·古铁雷斯）
第60分钟，他上场替换上半场点球破门的劳塔罗·马丁内斯，怒吼声更加响亮。
尽管梅西作为欧洲最佳球员八次获得金球奖，但从未在每届世界杯上以最佳射手的身份获得过金靴奖。这是他的第六次。
梅西目前在本届世界杯上打进了六个进球，领先基利安·姆巴佩、维尼修斯·儒尼奥尔和埃尔林·哈兰德两个。
阿根廷队的莱安德罗·帕雷德斯（左）、莱昂内尔·梅西（中）和亚历克西斯·麦克·阿利斯特于 2026 年 6 月 24 日星期三在堪萨斯州堪萨斯城为世界杯足球赛进行训练。（美联社照片/Charlie Riedel）
梅西在迈阿密国际队期间一直受到轻微的腿筋伤势的困扰，这使得他在世界杯前的比赛中表现不佳。
阿根廷队的淘汰赛将于周五在南佛罗里达州拉开帷幕，如果 La Albiceleste 队能够进入 7 月 19 日的决赛，那么在这场由 48 支球队组成的扩大锦标赛中，这将是 17 天内五场比赛中的第一场。
“今天他本来可以打 90 分钟，”斯卡罗尼说。“他希望他的队友有时间在球场上，并为即将发生的事情保护自己。他并没有过多考虑人们正在谈论的数字。”

item.title: 泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯要在麦迪逊广场花园结婚吗？我们所知道的
item.description:
AreTaylor Swift and Travis Kelcereally getting married at Madison Square Garden?
Frenzied speculation surrounding the superstar singer and football player’s upcoming wedding has spiked over the past few days as reports swirl that the two are getting married the first week in July at one of New York’s iconic landmarks.
Yet nearly a year after Kelce and Swift announcedtheir engagementwith the caption “Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married,” they have disclosed little about their plans.
Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce, right, is congratulated by Taylor Swift as they celebrate the Chiefs’ victory over the Buffalo Bills in the AFC Championship NFL football game, Jan. 26, 2025, in Kansas City, Mo. (AP Photo/Charlie Riedel, File)
Nothing has been publicly confirmed by the couple, despite The Associated Press’ multiple requests to Swift’s representative for comment.
Citing an entertainment industry executive and another person with knowledge of the matter,The New York Timesreported that the couple were planning a gathering of 100 people at Madison Square Garden — an arena that seats up to 19,500 people — on July 2 followed by a second event at the arena for about 1,000 guests on July 3.
The Associated Press has been unable to independently confirm those details, but there are no public events planned at the Garden from June 29 until a Bon Jovi concert on July 7.
Public records show that the city issued a permit for loading and unloading theatrical materials at the arena from June 29 to July 4. Winick Productions, a company that has produced red carpet events for the Grammy and Tony award shows and movie premieres, also applied for a permit to set up a canopy or tent outside the Garden for an event involving up to 999 people.
Meanwhile, just a few weeks prior, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani jokingly referenced the reports of Swift and Kelce’s wedding during apress conference. Mamdani was answering questions about safety during the World Cup when he said, “We know it coincides with July Fourth, America 250, Taylor Swift’s wedding all happening at the same time, and we are so excited to welcome the world here.”
Mamdani, however, said he was not invited to the wedding.
“I wish them a lovely wedding. I’ll listen to ‘Only the Young’ at home on my own,” he said, referencing one of Swift’s songs.
New York City police on horseback patrol the street by Madison Square Garden during Game 5 of the NBA Finals basketball series against the San Antonio Spurs, June 13, 2026, in New York. (AP Photo/Heather Khalifa, File)
Madison Square Garden may not scream “bridal,” but the venue is available for private rentals, advertising a banquet capacity for 1,250 — or 2,000 if you are only serving cocktails. And it has hosted weddings before. Sly Stone got married to Kathy Silva there in 1974 before thousands of fans. And more than 2,000 couples were wed in a mass ceremony at the Garden officiated bythe Rev. Sun Myung Moonin 1982.
Located above Penn Station, the busiest rail hub in the U.S., the Garden doesn’t scream “privacy” either.
But it does have guarded entrances, a secure garage and a lack of windows, which would allow Kelce, Swift and celebrity guests to stay out of sight of photographers or camera-equipped drones.
That need for privacy popped up earlier in June when a large tent appeared next door to Swift’s Watch Hill estate in Rhode Island. Despite organizers denying the event was for Swift, a wave of speculation bubbled up online as photographers and some Swifties headed to the town to see if they couldcatch a glimpse of a possible wedding.
Yet others have theorized that the MSG buzz could be an elaborate smoke screen to throw off attention to the couple’s real wedding plans. Swift did once write, “No, you can’t come to the wedding,” in her song “But Daddy I Love Him,” which some fans have been reupping lately as a reminder that the wedding isn’t supposed to be a public spectacle.
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce watch the second half of Game 3 in the Eastern Conference finals NBA basketball playoffs series between the New York Knicks and the Cleveland Cavaliers in Cleveland, May 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Tim Phillis, File)
Swift joked in October that “anyone I’ve ever talked to” would be invited to the wedding, telling Graham Norton that she believed “the only stressful weddings” are those that are small and force people to make aggressive cuts to the guest list.
Yet just who exactly will show up is to be determined. Aside from family, Kelce’s Kansas City Chiefs teammate Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, will likely be in attendance. For Swift, close friends like Selena Gomez, Abigail Anderson Berard,the Haim sisters,Emma Stone and Gigi Hadid will all likely attend.
Chiefs coach Andy Reid said he couldn’t talk about it when asked by The Associated Press if he was going to the wedding.
“If it’s like when I got married, my wife did everything, so I just kind of followed her lead on it, showed up, right? Maybe he’s doing more but he looks like he’s pretty focused in on this job here, too,” Reid said in early June.
Perhaps another clue why the week of the Fourth of July makes sense forSwift and Kelce’s weddingis that the popstar has long been known for throwing elaborate parties over the American holiday.
It wasn’t too long ago that fans dubbed her Fourth of July events as “Taymerica,” where celebrities showed up at her Rhode Island estate wearing red, white and blue swimsuits, waving American flags and eventually shared some social media photos with the public.
The timing also works with Kelce’s football schedule, given the tight end once joked on his “New Heights” podcast, “Don’t make my friends have to choose whether or not they have to sell their tickets that week.”
Associated Press writers Anthony Izaguirre and Maria Sherman in New York and David Skretta in Kansas City, Missouri, contributed reporting.

item.title: 走进乡村传奇人物艾伦·杰克逊的胜利压轴音乐会
item.description:
NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The time has come for country music superstarAlan Jacksonto hang up his signature Stetson hat.
The genre traditionalist from Newnan, Georgia, whose career kicked off in the 1980s and exploded shortly thereafter in the oft-cited ‘90s country wave with heartfelt songs for the working man who’d rather be drinking, or fishing, or ideally both, has sold over 60 million records across his storied career. And on Saturday night at Nissan Stadium in Nashville, he brought his touring career to an end.
The event titled “Last Call: One More for the Road — The Finale” was a triumphant swan song for the performer, a celebration of his life and career with some help from the artists he directly inspired.
The first two hours were made up of a marathon run of Jackson covers from some of the biggest names in contemporary country. And each performer had a personal story to share.Carrie Underwoodsang “Everything I Love” after revealing that Jackson was her first ever concert, in 1994 at the Tulsa State Fair. Thomas Rhett warmed up the crowd with “Small Town Southern Man,” an appropriate choice for a singer currently living the song’s lyrics — he’s a father to four girls.
The Texas-born and bredMiranda Lambertperformed “Dallas.” Lainey Wilson got the crowd moving with “Tall, Tall Trees.”
“It’s almost impossible to pick a favorite Alan Jackson song ... but I had to try,” said Luke Combs before launching into “Hard Hat and a Hammer.”
Each performer played with Jackson’s backing band, save forEric Church,who opted to cover “Someday” with just his voice and an acoustic guitar.
It was an All-Star night for one of country music’s most colossal voices. Other guest performers included Luke Bryan, Riley Green, Cody Johnson, Little Big Town, Jake Owen, Jon Pardi, Lee Ann Womack and a slew of super talented members of Jackson’s own family: Adam Wright, Big City Brian Wright and Carlisle Wright.
Five years ago, the 67-year-old music giant Jackson shared that he has a degenerative nerve condition that affects his balancecalled Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease,which he was first diagnosed with a decade prior. He said it was a genetic condition, and its effects on his ability to walk and perform had become more noticeable. One dollar of every ticket sold on Saturday night the CMT Research Foundation, an organization that funds research to find a cure for Charcot-Marie-Tooth.
When it was time for Jackson to hit the stage after 9:35 p.m. — after a storm delay of about an hour — he was met with ear-piercing cheers. The singer appeared stiff as he walked to his microphone, but once he picked up his guitar for the opener “Gone Country,” he was immediately back in action with that smoky baritone and timeless songs, though strumming was kept to a minimum.
“It’s overwhelming,” he addressed the crowd before assuring them he would not spend too much time on “that last show stuff … I’m not dead!”
The Country Music Hall of Famer ran through his best-known hits with real ferocity: “I Don’t Even Know Your Name” arrived quickly, as did “Livin’ on Love,” “Summertime Blues” and the moody “Midnight in Montgomery,” as the music videos for each played on a giant screen behind them.
He made it a point to walk from side to side of the stage, greeting each section while championing his band and the power of “real country music.”
“If anyone has lived the American dream,” he said later, while seated on a stool, “It’s me.”
Anecdotes flowed from there. He talked about writing “I’d Love You All Over Again” for his wife on their 10th wedding anniversary and how the radio from “Chasin’ that Neon Rainbow” is currently inthe Country Music Hall of Famemuseum. And he mentioned that “Drive (For Daddy Gene” was written after his father died.
An hour into his set, Jackson teased the audience by saying he needed some help for the next song. Out emergedGeorge Straitfor their collaborations “Designated Drink” and “Murder on Music Row.”
Then came an incredible run of hits: “Little Bitty,” “Country Boy,” “Good Time” and “Where Were You (When the World Stopped Turning)” among them, the latter written and recorded followingthe Sept. 11 terror attacks.
Blockbuster singles followed: “Don’t Rock the Jukebox,” “Remember When,” and “It’s Five O’Clock Somewhere,” the latter recording famously featuring thelate “Margaritaville” singer Jimmy Buffett.
Fireworks were launched into the sky for “Chattahoochee.”
Just because this is the end of Jackson’s touring career doesn’t mean it is the end of his music career. On Thursday, two days before the final concert blowout, Jackson released a country cover of Orleans’ “Still the One,” to celebrate his 50-year relationship with wife and high school sweetheart Denise Jackson. She was a cheerleader practicing a dance routine to the soft rock classic; he was instantly smitten.
For those who missed Jackson’s final bow, the show will be released later in the year as an NBC concert special. But for those who were in the stadium — in the middle of a huge storm — it was an unrepeatable and unmistakable night.

item.title: BET 奖：德鲁斯基将创造历史，成为最年轻的主持人，节目向劳伦·希尔 (Lauryn Hill) 和泰亚娜·泰勒 (Teyana Taylor) 致敬
item.description:
洛杉矶（美联社）——周日，德鲁斯基将创造历史，成为 BET 颁奖典礼最年轻的主持人，劳伦·希兰 (Lauryn Hill) 和泰亚娜·泰勒 (Teyana Taylor) 将与卡迪·B (Cardi B)、多奇 (Doechii) 和奎因·拉蒂法 (Queen Latifah) 等明星一起获奖，预计他们将登上舞台。
德鲁斯基将超越凯文·哈特 (Kevin Hart)，后者曾于 2011 年担任 BET 奖最年轻的主持人。
这位 31 岁的喜剧演员兼数字创作者将于晚上 8 点在洛杉矶孔雀剧院现场主持颁奖典礼。美东时间。
卡迪·布莱 (Cardi B) 以六项提名领跑今年的 BET 奖，而肯德里克·拉马 (Kendrick Lamar) 和科学家玛丽亚 (Mariah the Scientist) 各获得五项提名。Doechii、Doja Cat、Clipse、Teyana Taylor、Olivia Dean 和 Latto 分别获得四项提名。
年度最佳专辑包括 Cardi B 的《Am I the Drama?》、Clipse 的《Let God Sort Em Out》、J. Cole 的《The Fall-Off》、Leon Thomas 的《Mutt Deluxe: Heel》、Mariah the Scientist 的《Hearts Sold Splitly》、Tyler, the Creator 的《Don’t Tap the Glass》、Bruno Mars 的《The Romantic》以及 Wale 的《everything is a》。很多。”
今年几首最热门的歌曲也在争夺年度歌曲，包括肯德里克·拉马尔和 SZA 的《Luther》、莱昂·托马斯的《Mutt》、Doechii 的《Denial Is a River》和玛丽亚科学家的《Burning Blue》。
第一波表演者包括Cardi B、Common、Doechii、Don Toliver、French Montana、Jill Scott、Kehlani、Queen Latifah、Rick Ross、Tems、T.I.以及《战争与条约》。
嘻哈先锋 MC Lyte 再次担任节目播音员。
德鲁斯基预计将把他的即兴风格和互联网喜剧带到 BET 奖的舞台上。
他通过病毒式的小品成为娱乐圈中崛起最快的明星之一，然后扩展到爆满的喜剧巡演以及与 Drake 和 Snoop Dogg 等艺术家的合作，以及与 Tom Brady 和 Timothée Chalamet 等人物一起亮相。
“我是看着 BET 颁奖典礼长大的，”德鲁斯基说。“要知道在我之前主持的喜剧传奇人物设定了如此高的标准，我很感激能够成为历史的一部分。但我仍然将我的喜剧品牌带到舞台上。”
希尔将获得“活着的传奇偶像奖”，该奖项旨在表彰那些其工作在代代相传中仍然具有重要文化意义的先驱者。她最初是作为 Fugees 乐队的一员出现，然后发行了她具有里程碑意义的个人首张专辑《The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill》，这是一张挑战流派的专辑，成为嘻哈和 R&B 历史上最具影响力的唱片之一。
《BET》称希尔是“活着的传奇人物”，称赞她重塑了音乐，同时激励了一代又一代的艺术家。
出演《一场接一场》的泰勒将获得年度偶像奖，以表彰其影响力塑造当今文化的创造力。她已从一位多白金唱片艺术家发展成为一位屡获殊荣的演员、导演、制片人和编舞。
音乐高管西尔维娅·罗恩 (Sylvia Rhone) 将因其突破性的领导力和对音乐行业的持久影响而获得终极偶像奖。
罗纳创造了历史，成为第一位领导财富 500 强公司旗下大型唱片公司的黑人女性。在长达数十年的职业生涯中，她帮助培养了包括 Missy Elliott、Lil Wayne、Tracy Chapman、Brandy、Erykah Badu、Kid Cudi、Future、Travis Scott 和 Giveon 在内的艺术家。
该奖项的奖项类别包括针对创作者和内容系列的脉冲奖以及针对时尚文化影响力的时尚先锋奖。
